title: Soul Stuff Again split
wikitext:
[]: Soul Stuff (Again) [split]             Quote Post by TheLobe » Sat Apr 18, 2009 11:04 pm [ Split from here .] Axell wrote: hmm, if those are actual trees thats a big continuity error since as i said before, if you compare 3I to 2I there should be no living things left, nothing but tang.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Apr 18, 2009 11:20 pm TheLobe wrote: unless the trees are dead. plus trees dont have souls.

[]:             Quote Post by TheLobe » Sun Apr 19, 2009 2:06 am uh...cant understand ugh..BLAM (head explodes)

[]:             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Sun Apr 19, 2009 2:24 am Reichu wrote: TheLobe wrote: unless the trees are dead. plus trees dont have souls.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Apr 19, 2009 2:27 am Reichu wrote: There are other ways of figuring out that souls are a universal trait of lifeforms, as well.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Apr 19, 2009 3:35 am Mr. Tines wrote: Personally, I assumed the implication "trees = unaffected"

[]:             Quote Post by TheLobe » Sun Apr 19, 2009 7:29 am does this mean they get to be one with sheep. lucky bastards.

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Sun Apr 19, 2009 7:43 am Reichu wrote: Are the trees still soulless things that didn't Tang?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Apr 19, 2009 10:04 am Reichu wrote: "Red Earth". Trees. Think about it a little...

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Apr 19, 2009 10:17 am Mr. Tines: You just ignored all of the important parts of my post. Sometimes I really want to kill you, you know that...? The barren "Red Earth" is most likely intended to represent Earth after being totally nuked with an AATF -- lifeless, returned to the original state, "purified", just like Seele wanted. Red Earth Purification Ceremony and all. The idea I was trying to get across is that trees will emerge "from the Red Earth", but they will not inexplicably "coexist with it". EDIT: Crap, forgot this one: Shigeru: The Anti-AT Field from Lilith... it's expanding further! It's materializing! Makoto: Anti-AT Field critical limit exceeded! Shigeru: No...! If this continues, individual life-forms will be unable to maintain their separate shapes !

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Apr 19, 2009 10:25 am Since the topic seems to have shifted about 2 pages ago (with mod assistance no less ), I suggest renaming this thread. My thoughts on this whole 'red earth purification ceremony' business: I'm thinking everything got tanged. I don't know if Rei-tachi collected the souls of everything everywhere or if they were more choosy, thou I suspect the later. Judging by the comments of Seele about joining everything together, I think this is actually a departure from their scenario. I suspect that even with the AATF souls remained bound to their bodies unless they willingly left them, or were coaxed to do so by quantum magic Rei psychopomps. GNR's choice to unleash a wave of cross-blasts across the globe would be completely pointless destruction/symbolism unless there were a few holdouts who needed to be convinced via alternate methods. The 'trees return' can't be looked at separately from the whole scene, which is either symbolic of rebirth or a prophesy that humans will once again rebuild a modern society... eventually. I'm leaning towards the later, but only if the prophesy conveniently glossed over the decades or even centuries-long period of clan warfare that will happen before stable city-states reform and humans rediscover all the technological knowledge that will surely be lost in this new dark age. If it's taken to be a series of stages in the process then the GNR head moment has to be taken into account as happening after the trees portion of the event, and the destruction from ZOMG HUEG waves would be sufficient to eliminate any sort of useful plant or animal life that might have been waiting for Shinji in the Hakone area... so an all-tang diet for an extended period is still likely. Now, on the topic of souls... I have questions: 1) Where did they come from? Did Lilith supply them right up front as part of the easy-bake ecosystem or are they emergent properties that only came into existence when life developed on it's own from the LCL substrate? Does reproduction cause part of a parent's soul to divide in the creation of offspring or does a new soul develop on it's own? 2) Are souls quantitatively different between species? Are the souls of more complex lifeforms just different or are they simply the unification of purpose between individual cellular souls? What differentiates the soul of one specimen from another of the same species, or are they interchangeable? If principles of metaphysical biology were applied to transfer the souls of two people between bodies, would the soul dictate identity, or would the body in which it resides take precedence? 3) If souls remain attached to a corpse after death, do they simply linger until sufficient decomposition has occured and then leave intact? Might they not degrade over time as well with the decomposition process? Or, perhaps, do they linger in the body even after decomposition down to the inorganic stage, subdivided into near-oblivion but forever trapped within matter? 4) When a soul is consumed along with it's attached body, is it also digested and absorbed into the consumer's soul or does it remain in the byproducts? Do souls require nourishment, specifically that of other souls? If a person were to subsist on a diet of artificially created nutrients correctly balanced for all biological needs, would their soul wither away? 5) If part of a person truely does exist in part within the minds of others, does that mean that popularity causes the soul to degrade/subdivide at an increased rate? Are the Amish correct in believing a photograph steals part of a person's soul? Does this mean that fame literally creates effectively souless people while ascetic hermits remain most purely themselves?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Apr 19, 2009 10:34 am NemZ wrote: Now, on the topic of souls...

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Apr 19, 2009 11:00 am I shall have to schedule a first rewatch in 5 years at some point We don't see any, IIRC, tangification out of doors where there was any local vegetation to monitor, even in this scene SCENE: Outside NERV 1st soldier: S2 system level critical! Background: Abort operations! All units pull back at once! 2nd soldier: We... We can't maintain molecular attraction any longer! JSSDF Commander: Mission... failed...

[]:             Quote Post by Baz » Sun Apr 19, 2009 11:01 am @Nemz: Sounds like you're trying to revive our discussion from SSD's EoE Instrumentality thread. All of my comments here pre-suppose the NGE universe. NemZ wrote: Now, on the topic of souls...

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Apr 19, 2009 11:03 am @Reichu: I actually rather enjoyed that 'horrible' one, but if you don't want to go there with this thread I won't continue to push the issue. Then again... if that one's locked where else can such discussions go if anyone does have any pertinent thoughts on my questions? EDIT: Dang, they got in here before I replied. Um... sorry about that?

[]:             Quote Post by Baz » Sun Apr 19, 2009 11:20 am @Nemz: since Reichu and Tines are just rehashing an argument from years past, I don't think that you have any reason to apologize...

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Apr 19, 2009 2:36 pm NemZ wrote: I actually rather enjoyed that 'horrible' one

[]:             Quote Post by Baz » Sun Apr 19, 2009 2:50 pm @Reichu: You always go about things the hard way, by gathering evidence and using it to form a cohesive theory. It is so much easier to just start with a pet theory and then use it to explain the evidence*. _________________ *Now what do we call that again? Giving alms to the answer or something like that...

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Apr 19, 2009 5:35 pm Reichu wrote: Yes. Yes, I know.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Apr 19, 2009 5:40 pm Baz wrote: just rehashing an argument from years past
